narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—putting your heart into law school applications only to face this outcome feels deeply unfair, and I'm truly sorry you're going through it. Our prediction model looked at your profile holistically, like piecing together chapters of your academic story, and it foresaw a 41% chance of not passing the bar exam, which tipped the scales against admission. The biggest chapter that weighed on this was your Year 3 decile of 3.0, signaling a dip in performance that the model saw as the strongest pull toward struggle ahead. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 31.0, which didn't quite stand out in the crowd of high achievers the model associates with bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 4.0 added to that narrative of uneven momentum early on, making it the third most influential factor nudging toward this outcome. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.7, it couldn't fully counterbalance those trends, pulling somewhat toward failure in the model's view. Your upper-middle family income, while a strength in many ways, surprisingly aligned with patterns the model links to bar challenges here. Elements like being a white female pursuing full-time studies rounded out the picture, each contributing a subtle tug in the same direction. Together, these painted a portrait of someone whose path might hit more hurdles than smooth sailing to the bar. It's not a judgment on your potential or drive—many with similar starts have defied odds through targeted changes like boosting study strategies or retaking the LSAT. This is just one data-driven snapshot meant to guide tough decisions, and I hope it empowers you to refine your journey ahead. You've got resilience in you; keep pushing.
